channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Indian Government Advises Nationals in Iran to Return Home Due to Escalating Tensions
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) of India has issued a strong advisory urging around 7,500 Indian citizens currently residing in Iran to depart the country without delay. The directive comes ami...

item.title: Jasveen Sangha faces sentencing for supplying fatal ketamine to Matthew Perry
item.description: Jasveen Sangha, a 42-year-old Los Angeles woman known as the ‘Ketamine Queen’ for her high-end drug operation, is scheduled for sentencing on April 8, 2026, in connection with Matthew Perry’s 2023 ove...

item.title: Islamabad announces two-day closure as US-Iran peace talks scheduled
item.description: The Islamabad administration has declared April 9 and 10, 2026 (Thursday and Friday), as local holidays throughout the capital territory. According to the official notification, all educational instit...

item.title: PTI Postpones April 9 Rally Following Govt Talks Ahead of US-Iran Negotiations
item.description: Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) has decided to postpone its planned rally on April 9 at Liaquat Bagh in Rawalpindi. This decision came after discussions between federal government representatives and o...

item.title: Comparing Wild Casino and Donbet: Benefits and Features for US Gamblers
item.description: This analysis provides a detailed comparison of two leading offshore gambling platforms, Wild Casino and Donbet, specifically catering to US players. Wild Casino, with its established presence spannin...

item.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off episode featuring Scott Mills removed from airing after BBC presenter’s departure
item.description: The final episode of 'The Great Celebrity Bake Off' has been withdrawn from airing, following controversy surrounding one of its hosts, Scott Mills, who was recently dismissed by the BBC. Mills, a pro...

item.title: Parliament Summons Energy CS Wandayi Over Questions on Fuel Quality and Supply

item.title: Bangladesh FM Meets Indian Counterpart to Discuss Bilateral Issues
item.description: Bangladesh’s Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman made an official visit to New Delhi on Wednesday, marking the most senior diplomatic engagement since the 2024 political upheaval that ousted former Prime...

item.title: Man arrested in Bradford over 2004 murder of Leeds businessman John Luper
item.description: West Yorkshire Police have arrested a 55-year-old man in Bradford on suspicion of murdering Leeds businessman John Luper, who was killed during a robbery at his home in Alwoodley on 16 February 2004. ...

item.title: Pakistan emerges as key mediator in US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: Pakistan has gained significant international recognition for brokering a ceasefire between the United States and Iran. Following a tense standoff in which US President Donald Trump issued a stark ult...

item.title: Former soldier Ben Roberts-Smith arrested on multiple murder charges
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith, one of Australia's most decorated former soldiers, has been arrested by the Australian Federal Police on suspicion of five counts of murder. The arrest took place at Sydney Airport,...

item.title: Pakistan’s Quiet Diplomacy Brings US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: Over the past fortnight, Pakistan played a discreet but pivotal role in mediating a ceasefire between the United States and Iran. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar conducted...

item.title: Long Island Man Rex Heuermann Expected to Plead Guilty to Seven Gilgo Beach Murders
item.description: Rex Heuermann, a 62-year-old Long Island architect and father of two, is set to plead guilty to the murders of seven women in the notorious Gilgo Beach serial killings. The crimes, spanning 17 years, ...

item.title: Kirk Cousins joins Raiders to reunite with former coaches, including Klint Kubiak
item.description: Kirk Cousins, the quarterback for the Las Vegas Raiders, explained that one of the primary reasons he chose to join the team was his desire to reunite with head coach Klint Kubiak. Cousins previously ...

item.title: Divorce Rates Surge in Tanzania, Prompting Calls for Reconciliation Board Support
item.description: Divorce cases in Tanzania have seen a significant and concerning increase, jumping from 711 in 2023 to a worrying 1,172 in 2025. This alarming trend, as revealed by Deputy Permanent Secretary Franklin...

item.title: Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation Tackles Rising Alcohol Theft with Enhanced Security
item.description: The Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation (NSLC) is facing a significant challenge as alcohol theft continues to rise across the province. The incidents have prompted the NSLC to implement various measures a...

item.title: Tensions Flare Between Lawmakers During MP Kiaraho’s Memorial Service
item.description: During the memorial service for the late MP Kiaraho, a tense confrontation broke out between two central Kenya leaders — MP Thuku and Senator Methu. The event, held to honour the fallen lawmaker’s ser...

item.title: Bacolod Court Declares Four Yanson Siblings Fugitives, Intensifies Family Feud
item.description: The long-running family dispute within the Yanson clan in Bacolod City has taken a dramatic turn. A local court has declared four siblings – Roy, Ricardo Jr., Emily, and Ma. Lourdes Celina, collective...

item.title: Psychology behind premium food purchases in uncertain economic times
item.description: Despite economic anxiety and declining consumer confidence in the United States, some premium food items, such as $22 smoothies from Erewhon, continue to thrive. Wealthier consumers are sustaining thi...

item.title: Commercial shipping slowly resumes through the Strait of Hormuz after temporary ceasefire
item.description: After weeks of restricted maritime movement, two ships have successfully passed through the Strait of Hormuz following an agreement between Iran and the United States to observe a two-week ceasefire. ...
